body.title: Consequence check: agent taxonomy + registry field
body.result: All 4 predicted effects annotated: (1) class inflation — CLEAR, no new classes, concept node exists, latent risk from profile YAMLs lacking agent_class; (2) misclassification — CLEAR, all 7 device declarations correct; (3) Librarian confusion — CLEAR, platform-utility annotation explicit in code and palace; (4) Granny ignores agent_class — OCCURRED (by design), dispatch log and routing logic don't use agent_class, intentional deferral per decision doc
body.description:
**What changed:** Three-class agent taxonomy (utility/specialized/general) added to vocabulary and registry. Every device now self-declares its class.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Class inflation: future developers add new classes without updating the concept node, causing vocabulary drift
- Misclassification: a device claims 'general' to get broader routing even though it's utility-scoped
- Librarian confusion: 'utility with platform annotation' is ambiguous; someone classifies it as 'specialized' and it diverges from intent
- Granny routing assumption: Granny code somewhere assumes all non-CC workers are 'worker' tier and ignores agent_class — field exists but is never used

**Signals to watch:**
- New device PRs that don't include agent_class declaration
- Registry listing where agent_class is missing or 'unknown'
- Granny dispatch logs — does class appear in GRANNY_DISPATCH channel posts?

**Gate condition:** Check when T-device-registry-agent-class closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** Observation ticket — done when each predicted effect is annotated as occurred / clear / partial
